skill.name: functionality-audit
skill.description: Guides AI agents through sandboxed execution testing, bug reproduction, debugging, and verification reports for generated or modified code.
skill.summary: Validate code behavior with sandbox tests, failure analysis, and remediation guidance.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: The static weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and Windows SAM findings are false positives caused by documentation terms such as systematic debugging, network isolation, and AWS SAM. No prompt injection attempt, malware intent, credential collection, or executable payload was found. The main residual risk is operational: the skill encourages agents to run and inspect user code, so sandbox boundaries are important.

low_findings.title: Operational Risk from Executing User Code
low_findings.description: The skill is documentation only, but it directs agents to create sandboxes, execute code, monitor side effects, and use test tools. This is legitimate for functionality validation, but untrusted code execution can affect files, services, or credentials if isolation is weak.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines clearly describe sandbox creation, code execution monitoring, and specific testing tools. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the same text also calls for isolation and safe testing.

low_findings.title: Network and Cloud Testing Require Boundaries
low_findings.description: The skill discusses network isolation, isolated Docker networks, and real cloud sandboxes. These are not reconnaissance or exfiltration instructions, but users should restrict network access and avoid production credentials during tests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines explicitly mention network isolation and cloud sandboxes. I found no evidence that the skill instructs scanning, credential use, or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, network reconnaissance, and Windows SAM hits occur in prose or diagram labels. No executable cryptography, host reconnaissance, network scan, or Windows SAM database access appears in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed repository contains only a Markdown guide and a DOT process diagram. The suspicious terms appear as documentation text, including AWS SAM, not as code that can access sensitive resources.

content.value_statement: Generated code can look correct while failing under real inputs. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through sandbox testing, debugging, and verification reporting.
actual_capabilities: Creates a structured process for testing generated or modified code in isolated environments.
Guides test case design for normal paths, edge cases, invalid inputs, and stress conditions.
Directs agents to compare actual outputs against expected behavior and invariants.
Provides a debugging workflow for reproducing failures and identifying root causes.
Defines report sections for test results, bug lists, remediation tracking, and recommendations.
Recommends common Python, JavaScript, container, and cloud sandbox testing tools.
limitations: It is a methodology guide, not an automated test runner.
It needs clear expected behavior from the user or project documentation.
It may require local dependencies, containers, or cloud emulators to be useful.
It cannot prove correctness for behavior that was not specified or tested.

use_cases.title: Verify AI-Generated Code
use_cases.description: Run realistic tests before merging code produced by an AI assistant.

use_cases.title: Investigate Failing Features
use_cases.description: Reproduce a bug, isolate the failing path, and document a tested fix.

use_cases.title: Prepare Release Validation
use_cases.description: Create final functionality checks and regression evidence before deployment.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Functionality Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner validation after a small code change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the functionality-audit skill to test these files. Verify the expected behavior I describe, run safe local checks, and summarize pass or fail results.

prompt_templates.title: Edge Case Test Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Broader validation when expected behavior is known.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use functionality-audit to design and run test cases for normal inputs, invalid inputs, boundary values, and regression risks. Report any failures with likely causes.

prompt_templates.title: Bug Reproduction and Fix Verification
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate debugging for a known issue.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use functionality-audit to reproduce this bug in an isolated environment, identify the root cause, apply a focused fix, and verify the original failure no longer occurs.

prompt_templates.title: Release Readiness Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use functionality-audit for a release readiness pass. Build a sandbox, run representative integration checks, document residual risks, and recommend follow-up tests.

output_examples.input: Audit a new parser that should reject malformed records and preserve valid rows.
output_examples.output: The report lists tested valid records, malformed inputs, observed failures, root cause notes, and recommended parser fixes.

output_examples.input: Verify a JavaScript component after changing asynchronous loading behavior.
output_examples.output: The output summarizes successful render checks, timing failures, affected states, and regression tests to add.

output_examples.input: Check whether a Dockerized service still works after dependency upgrades.
output_examples.output: The audit describes container setup, service health checks, failed integration paths, and deployment blockers.

best_practices: Provide expected behavior, sample inputs, and known edge cases before testing starts.
Run untrusted code only inside isolated sandboxes with limited secrets and network access.
Keep test evidence and remediation notes with the code change.
anti_patterns: Do not treat passing syntax checks as proof that code works.
Do not run unknown code against production services or credentials.
Do not patch bugs before reproducing and understanding the failure.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It gives Claude, Codex, or Claude Code a structured workflow for planning, running, and reporting tests.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it after code generation, bug fixes, integrations, or release preparation when behavior must be verified.

faq.question: What inputs should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide file paths, expected behavior, sample data, dependencies, known failures, and any unsafe systems to avoid.

faq.question: Can it test cloud applications?
faq.answer: Yes, but it should use emulators, mock services, or isolated cloud sandboxes instead of production resources.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted code?
faq.answer: It can help test untrusted code only when strong sandboxing, secret isolation, and network limits are used.

faq.question: What does the final report include?
faq.answer: It includes test outcomes, bugs found, root cause notes, fixes verified, and recommendations for future testing.
